Private & Confidential — Cyber Incident Report for Bramley & Penrith Group plc; prepared under UK MAR Article 17 delay.
On 05 June 2026 at 02:14 BST we identified unauthorised credential use against the legacy “VistaPay” customer portal operated by our subsidiary Calder Analytics Ltd.
Forensic logs indicate exfiltration of a subset of 8,400 pseudonymised customer profile records and intermittent failure of the payments reconciliation module from 02:11–04:38 BST; no direct customer balances were altered.
The outage delayed settlement of approximately £42m equivalent in merchant funds by up to one business day; preliminary modelling suggests a potential EBITDA impact of 6–8% for FY26 from remediation, penalties, and lost volume, which may be material at issuer level.
This constitutes inside information for the purposes of UK MAR; disclosure is currently delayed on the basis that immediate notification would prejudice our incident response and confidentiality is being maintained without risk of the market being misled.
We are in a closed period ahead of our half-year results (scheduled 18 July 2026), so all PDMR and employee dealing remains prohibited and the standing blackout window is extended until we publish via a regulatory information service.
Selective disclosure is prohibited: do not brief customers, analysts, or lenders beyond approved lines; market soundings are not authorised, and any engagement with vendors must be under NDA and limited to operational detail only.
The insider list must be updated as of 06 June 2026 to include Ms. Priya K. Dhillon (CRO, priya.dhillon@bramleypenrith.co.uk), Dr. Saira Malik (CISO, saira.malik@bramleypenrith.co.uk), and Mr. Alistair Keane (CFO, alistair.keane@bramleypenrith.co.uk).
As noted in our public RIS announcement on 20 May 2026 (“Planned VistaPay Migration Timeline”), we previously stated there was no expected disruption to customer services during Q2; that statement is no longer current and will be corrected when we release a holding statement.
We are preparing a holding announcement for publication via a regulatory information service by 17:00 BST on 07 June 2026, subject to continued maintenance of confidentiality and stabilisation of core services.
Notifications to the competent data protection authority will be made within the statutory period; the draft to the UK Information Rights Office is under internal review and will not be submitted before our RIS unless legally required.
For avoidance of doubt, the sustainability report posted on 10 January 2026 and a 12 April 2026 press release about branch refurbishments are unrelated and contain no inside information.
Early media/social posts and a third-party uptime monitor referencing the VistaPay portal outage are not issuer communications; staff must not comment publicly or “confirm details on background.”
No cash movement has occurred outside normal reconciliations, and no employee payroll data is involved; card token vaults at our vendor Harcourt Gate Hosting Ltd were not accessed.
Registrar instructions: Albion Registrars Ltd have been told not to process exercises under the employee share plan until we cleanse the market via RIS and lift the blackout.
Queries should be directed to the Incident Desk (security@bramleypenrith.co.uk) or Investor Relations (ir@bramleypenrith.co.uk); legal questions should be escalated to Ms. Dhillon via +44 7000 111222.
